 {"id":"bd-2k8w","title":"demo_showcase: tree showcase scene","description":"Create a tree scene (deployment plan / dependency graph) demonstrating guide styles, icons, collapsed nodes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltCanyon","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:14.381218326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:25:47.922400867Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:25:47.922338741Z","close_reason":"Tree scene already implemented - deployment plan, service deps, guide styles, collapsed nodes all working","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","tree"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k8w","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2k8w","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2k8w","depends_on_id":"bd-2175","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":35,"issue_id":"bd-2k8w","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Tree content

Use a tree to present something users actually do:
- deployment plan steps and sub-steps
- service dependency graph
- file structure for a release artifact

Demonstrate:
- guides variants
- icons/emoji
- collapsed nodes

## Acceptance criteria

- Tree integrates with theme styles (guide style + node highlight).

 {"id":"bd-2k90","title":"demo_showcase: non-TTY smoke verification","description":"Add a CI-friendly run path and verify piping (`... | cat`) completes without blocking or infinite output.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:48.701197276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:19:18.988895803Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:19:18.988833717Z","close_reason":"Added 5 piped output verification tests: scene completion, readable text, no blocking pager, bounded output, quick mode performance","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k90","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2k90","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2k90","depends_on_id":"bd-3bid","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"bd-2k90","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What to check

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing | cat` finishes quickly.
- No prompt/pager attempts.
- Live animation is disabled or bounded.

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is finite and readable.
- Exit code 0 for successful run.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:55Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-2kr0","title":"Constrain renderable parity (rich.constrain)","description":"Implement Python Rich `rich.constrain` parity.

Scope:
- Add a `Constrain` renderable that constrains child renderable width (min/max or exact), integrating with measurement.
- Ensure panels/layouts can use it to enforce stable widths.

Acceptance criteria:
- `Constrain` exists as a renderable and is exported in prelude.
- Unit + e2e tests cover measurement + rendering.
- Add a Python conformance fixture for a constrained renderable to lock behavior.

Likely files:
- `src/renderables/` (new file only if justified)
- `src/measure.rs`
- `src/console.rs`","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:17:24.815893359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:17:24.836990815Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kr0","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:17:24.836954217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2kr0","title":"Constrain renderable parity (rich.constrain)","description":"Implement Python Rich `rich.constrain` parity.

Scope:
- Add a `Constrain` renderable that constrains child renderable width (min/max or exact), integrating with measurement.
- Ensure panels/layouts can use it to enforce stable widths.

Acceptance criteria:
- `Constrain` exists as a renderable and is exported in prelude.
- Unit + e2e tests cover measurement + rendering.
- Add a Python conformance fixture for a constrained renderable to lock behavior.

Likely files:
- `src/renderables/` (new file only if justified)
- `src/measure.rs`
- `src/console.rs`","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:17:24.815893359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:13:06.168246570Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kr0","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:17:24.836954217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kti","title":"Layout scene centered hero text not visually centered","description":"## Description
In the layout scene 'Horizontal Alignment' demo, the centered hero block text is not actually centered for the terminal width.

## Actual Behavior
```
                Nebula Deploy
            Production-ready in minutes
```
These lines appear left-aligned or partially centered, not fully centered for the display.

## Expected Behavior
Text should be horizontally centered across the full available width.

## Files
- src/bin/demo_showcase/layout_scene.rs:95-106","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-29T00:21:35.701570752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:43:09.010750518Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:43:09.010609205Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Hero text now centers relative to console width (capped at 100 cols). Commit 6a4b43b.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Target: 15+ tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:40.130943145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:34:23.756466183Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:34:23.756329358Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2l3t","depends_on_id":"bd-25hz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2llx","title":"Regression test: Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup()","description":"## Problem
Cell::new() was silently NOT parsing markup - users expected Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") to work but it didn't. We added Cell::from_markup() but need regression tests to prevent this from happening again and document the difference clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") does NOT parse markup (expected behavior, documents it)
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::from_markup("[bold]text[/]") DOES parse markup
- [ ] Test the difference in output between the two methods
- [ ] Add doc comments explaining when to use each method

## Implementation Notes
- The tests should be clear about the EXPECTED difference between the two methods
- This documents API behavior and prevents confusion

## Files to modify
- src/renderables/table.rs - improve doc comments
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add regression tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:27.981429151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610863384Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610835742Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 6 regression tests documenting Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup() difference. Updated Cell::new() doc comments with clear examples. All tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

@@ -64,6 +64,7 @@ This document is the source of truth. If code or docs change, update this file,
 | Emoji | Emoji renderable | Implemented | `src/renderables/emoji.rs` | Unit tests |
 | Padding | Padding | Implemented | `src/renderables/padding.rs` | Golden tests |
 | Align | Alignment | Implemented | `src/renderables/align.rs` | Golden tests |
+| Constrain | Constrain renderable width (`rich.constrain`) | Implemented | `src/renderables/constrain.rs` | Python fixture conformance: `constrain/rule_width_10`, `constrain/none_passthrough`. |
 | Pretty / Inspect | Debug-based pretty printing + type inspection | Implemented | `src/renderables/pretty.rs` | Unit tests + snapshots in `src/renderables/snapshots/` |
 | Traceback | Traceback rendering + `Console::print_exception` | Implemented | `src/renderables/traceback.rs` | Deterministic explicit frames + Python fixture conformance (`traceback/basic`). Code context via `extra_lines` + `source_context` (from file or embedded). Automatic Rust backtrace capture via `Traceback::capture()` (requires `backtrace` feature). Locals rendering supported when provided explicitly (`TracebackFrame::locals`, `Traceback::show_locals`). |
 | Syntax | Syntax highlighting | Partial | `src/renderables/syntax.rs` (feature `syntax`) | Python fixture conformance (`syntax/basic`, `syntax/no_terminal`) covers plain layout parity; ANSI differs vs Python Rich (syntect vs Pygments). Custom syntect theme/syntax loading is implemented (`Syntax::theme_set`, `Syntax::syntax_set`, `load_themes_from_folder`, `load_syntaxes_from_folder`) and covered by `tests/e2e_syntax.rs`. |

+    if kind == "constrain":
+        from rich.constrain import Constrain  # type: ignore
+
+        child_kind = inp.get("child_kind", "rule")
+        child_input = inp.get("child_input", {})
+        width = inp.get("width", 80)
+
+        child_case = {"kind": child_kind, "input": child_input}
+        child = build_renderable(child_case)
+        return Constrain(child, width=width)
+
     if kind == "rule":
         return Rule(inp.get("title", ""), characters=inp.get("character", "─"), align=inp.get("align", "center"))
